title: That God does not easily pardon idolaters.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That God does not easily pardon idolaters.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moses in Exodus prays for the people, and does not obtain his prayer, saying: “I pray, O Lord, this people hath sinned a great sin.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: They have made them gods of gold.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And now, if Thou forgivest them their sin, forgive it; but if not, blot me out of the book which Thou hast written.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And the Lord said unto Moses, If any one hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Moreover, when Jeremiah besought for the people, the Lord speaks to him, saying: “And pray not thou for this people, and entreat not for them in prayer and supplication; because I will not hear in the time wherein they shall call upon me in the time of their affliction.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Ezekiel also denounces this same anger of God upon those who sin against God, and says: “And the word of the Lord came unto me, saying, Son of man, whatsoever land sinneth against me, by committing an offence, I will stretch forth mine hand upon it, and will crush the support of the bread thereof; and I will send into it famine, and I will take away from it man and beast.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And though these three men were in the midst of it, Noah, Daniel, and Job, they shall not deliver sons nor daughters; they themselves only shall be delivered.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Likewise in the first book of Kings: “If a man sin by offending against another, they shall beseech the Lord for him; but if a man sin against God, who shall entreat for him?”
